/blog/why-faster-isn-t-always-better.title: In the wild  - learning to observe - LaunchFolio Portfolio Framer Template
/blog/why-faster-isn-t-always-better.description: A reflection on wildlife and bird photography as a practice of observation—shaping patience, awareness, and the way I understand environments.
h1: In the wild  - learning to observe
p: In the wild  - learning to observe
A reflection on wildlife and bird photography as a practice of observation—shaping patience, awareness, and the way I understand environments.

There’s a different kind of stillness in the wild—one that demands patience rather than movement. My interest in wildlife and bird photography began not with the intention to capture, but to observe. Over time, it became a practice of slowing down, paying attention, and learning to notice what often goes unseen.

Wildlife photography is rarely about the perfect shot. It is about waiting—sometimes for hours—with no guarantee of an outcome. In that waiting, I’ve learned to be present in a way that everyday life rarely allows.

Birds, especially, have shaped this experience. Their unpredictability, subtle movements, and sensitivity to their surroundings require a certain level of awareness. You begin to notice patterns—how they move, where they pause, what triggers flight. It becomes less about chasing a frame and more about understanding behavior.
There’s also a quiet discipline to it. Respecting distance, adapting to the environment, and accepting that not every moment is meant to be captured. Some are simply meant to be experienced.
Over time, this interest has extended beyond photography itself. It has influenced how I observe details, how I interpret environments, and how I stay patient through uncertainty.

Wildlife doesn’t follow a script. And that unpredictability has taught me to adapt—to be attentive without being intrusive, to wait without forcing outcomes, and to appreciate the process as much as the result.
This way of thinking naturally carries into my work as a designer. Observation becomes deeper, patterns become clearer, and decisions become more intentional.
I may not always return with the perfect photograph, but I always return with a sharper sense of awareness. And that, more than anything, is what keeps me going back.

/blog/designing-for-human-connection.title: In stillness - a practice that stayed - LaunchFolio Portfolio Framer Template
/blog/designing-for-human-connection.description: A journey with yoga from early practice to a deeper way of living shaping awareness, balance, and presence over time.
h1: In stillness - a practice that stayed
p: In stillness - a practice that stayed
A journey with yoga from early practice to a deeper way of living shaping awareness, balance, and presence over time.

Not everything that shapes us begins with intention. My journey with yoga started early, during my school days, without a clear understanding of what it truly meant. It was just something I practiced. Over time, however, it evolved into something far more meaningful—a practice that brought clarity, balance, and a deeper sense of awareness.

It was during my engineering years that yoga became more than just a routine. What began as a random introduction—friends pulling me into a yoga club called YUJ for Life—gradually turned into a space where I spent a significant part of my time.

The shift was subtle but important. Yoga moved from being a physical activity to a more intentional practice. Breathing, posture, and awareness were no longer separate elements—they became interconnected.

As I spent more time with the practice and the community, my role evolved as well. By the time I graduated, I had taken on the responsibility of serving as the Vice President of the club. It wasn’t just a position—it was an opportunity to contribute, guide, and stay deeply engaged with something that had started shaping my mindset.
What began within structured sessions didn’t stay confined to them.

Over time, yoga found its way into how I experience the outdoors as well. Whether it’s reaching a summit during a trek or finding a quiet spot at a peak, I often find myself sitting down, taking a moment to pause, and simply being present.

Practicing meditation in those spaces brings a different kind of clarity—one that’s shaped by both stillness and surroundings. Occasionally, I also explore asanas in these environments, not as a performance, but as a way to stay connected to the moment.
Yoga, for me, is no longer something I “do” at a specific time or place. It has become a way of approaching things—with awareness, patience, and a sense of balance.
It started early, took shape over time, and continues to evolve—quietly, but consistently.

/blog/how-designers-and-developers-can-a.title: Trek, Hike, Mountains I am always in! - LaunchFolio Portfolio Framer Template
/blog/how-designers-and-developers-can-a.description: A reflection on how trekking shaped my perspective—through experiences like Kedarkantha and beyond, exploring not just mountains, but mindset, presence, and growth.
h1: Trek, Hike, Mountains I am always in!
p: Trek, Hike, Mountains I am always in!
A reflection on how trekking shaped my perspective—through experiences like Kedarkantha and beyond, exploring not just mountains, but mindset, presence, and growth.

There’s a certain clarity I find in the mountains that’s hard to replicate anywhere else. What began as an occasional break from routine gradually became a space where I could think more clearly, observe more deeply, and understand myself better. Trekking, for me, is not just a physical journey—it’s an exercise in presence, patience, and perspective.
Kedarkantha was not planned with high expectations. It was, in many ways, just another trek I decided to take. But the experience turned out to be far more significant than I had anticipated.

Like most unfamiliar challenges, it began with hesitation. There was a constant internal dialogue—questioning my readiness, my endurance, and whether I would be able to keep up. But as the trek progressed, that uncertainty slowly gave way to a quiet sense of confidence.
The mountains have a way of simplifying things. The focus shifts from overthinking to simply taking the next step.

What stood out most during this journey was not just the terrain, but the shift in mindset. At one point, I found myself stepping into a leadership role for the group, even if briefly and under guidance. It was unexpected, but it revealed how quickly we can adapt when we allow ourselves to be present in the moment.

Another aspect that stayed with me was the intentional disconnection. With my phone on flight mode, there were no distractions—only conversations, observations, and time to reflect. It created a sense of awareness that is often missing in everyday life.

Reaching the summit at 12,500 feet was undoubtedly rewarding, but what made it meaningful was everything that led up to it—the uncertainty at the start, the gradual progress, and the collective effort of the group. The summit was not just a destination; it was a culmination of the journey.

While Kedarkantha stands out as a defining experience, it is part of a much larger journey. Over time, I’ve explored a range of terrains and trails—each offering a different kind of challenge and learning.

From the dense forests of Kodachadri and Kudremukh, to the demanding climb of Kumara Parvatha, to experiences beyond Karnataka like the Peb trek (Matheran region) and the Laka Glacier trail leading towards Indrahar Pass via Triund—each trek has added a new layer to how I understand both the outdoors and myself.

Closer to home, shorter hikes like Ethina Bhuja, Pandava Betta, and Kunti Betta (Mandya) have been equally meaningful in their own way—reminding me that it’s not always about the scale of the mountain, but the intent to step out and explore.

These journeys, along with many more hikes, have gradually shaped how I approach challenges—staying patient through uncertainty, adapting to changing conditions, and focusing on the process rather than just the outcome.

Over time, this mindset has extended into my work as a designer as well. Trekking has taught me to observe more carefully, embrace ambiguity, and understand that meaningful experiences—whether in the mountains or in design—are built step by step.
The mountains may differ, and the routes may change, but the intent remains the same. If there’s an opportunity to be out there, I know I’ll take it.

/projects/axiom.title: LaunchNow Portfolio Framer Template - Never lose your parking spot—even without network.
/projects/axiom.description: Designing intuitive digital experiences by simplifying complex problems—focusing on clarity, usability, and real-world impact across every product I build.
h1: Never lose your parking spot—even without network.
p: Designing a parking experience that works even when networks don’t—helping users navigate multi-story buildings and locate their exact parking space with clarity and ease.

By combining offline maps, indoor navigation, and QR-based interactions, the solution creates a seamless connection between users and parking management—turning a frustrating process into a guided journey.

Designing a parking experience that works seamlessly even in low-network, multi-story buildings—helping users book, navigate, and locate their exact parking space without relying on unstable GPS signals.

By combining indoor navigation, offline maps, and QR-based verification, the solution bridges the gap between car owners and parking management—making the entire journey from entry to exit smooth, predictable, and stress-free.
By the end of this project, users no longer had to circle around parking floors trying to locate their assigned spot.
Navigate directly to the parking entrance
Check in seamlessly using a QR code
Follow guided directions to their exact parking slot
Exit without confusion through a simple scan-and-pay flow
Even in low-network conditions, the experience remained reliable—removing one of the most frustrating parts of urban driving.
Manual coordination → Structured system for both users and management
For users, it became about clarity and confidence.For management, it introduced control and visibility.
Parking in multi-story buildings often breaks down at the worst moment—right when users need precision.
GPS signals drop or become inaccurate indoors
Users struggle to locate assigned parking spaces
Time is wasted navigating floors without clarity
Management lacks tools to guide and verify users efficiently
The system had all the components—but no unified experience to connect them.
This project wasn’t just about UI—it was about designing for constraints.
The core challenge:How do you create a reliable navigation experience where network and GPS cannot be trusted?
Indoor positioning systems (like iBeacon, UWB, or similar technologies)
QR-based checkpoints for verification and flow continuity
This ensured the experience doesn’t break—even when connectivity does.
The experience was designed as a continuous flow:
Users select or add their vehicle
Navigate to the parking entrance via map
A QR code is generated and scanned for verification
Entry is logged and parking session begins
Users are guided through indoor navigation
Visual cues help them reach the exact allotted space
At every step, the system connects user actions with management visibility.
A key part of the design was solving for both sides:
This ensured the solution wasn’t just usable—but operationally viable.
Seamless across entry, parking, and exit
The experience shifts from “Where do I go?”to “I know exactly where I’m going.”

/projects/axiom-copy.title: LaunchNow Portfolio Framer Template - From breakdown stress to seamless service—anytime, anywhere.
/projects/axiom-copy.description: Designing intuitive digital experiences by simplifying complex problems—focusing on clarity, usability, and real-world impact across every product I build.
h1: From breakdown stress to seamless service—anytime, anywhere.
h2: Solving for moments that matter most
p: Designing an end-to-end automobile service platform that simplifies booking, tracking, and managing vehicle services—especially during breakdown scenarios where reliability and speed are critical.

By combining service booking, real-time tracking, pickup & drop support, and transparent communication, the solution transforms a traditionally uncertain experience into one that is structured, predictable, and user-focused.

Designing an online platform that simplifies how people book, track, and manage their automobile services—especially during unexpected breakdowns where time, availability, and support become critical.

The solution focuses on creating a reliable, end-to-end experience that not only helps users book services easily but also ensures transparency through tracking, timely updates, and accessible support—bridging the gap between vehicle owners and service providers.
Vehicle issues rarely happen at convenient times.
By the end of this project, users could:
Access pickup & drop facilities without hassle
Track their vehicle service in real-time
Receive timely updates instead of waiting blindly
Get support when something goes wrong
Avoid delays caused by spare parts or poor coordination
What was once a stressful, uncertain experience became predictable and manageable.
Users moved from “What’s happening with my vehicle?”to “I know exactly what’s going on.”
From research and user conversations, a few consistent pain points emerged:
Difficulty finding mechanics during sudden breakdowns
Long waiting times with no visibility or updates
Lack of customer support and accountability
No access to pickup & drop services
Delays due to spare parts unavailability
Poor time management across service workflows
There was a clear gap between service availability and user expectations.
Over 14 weeks, the project was built on strong research foundations:
Focused on real experiences, frustrations, and expectations
Explored both existing platform usage and offline behaviors
Users don’t just want a way to book services—they want:
Reliability, visibility, and control during uncertain situations.
The platform was designed as a complete service ecosystem, not just a booking tool.
To ensure usability and clarity, the process included:
Persona & Empathy Mapping to understand users deeply
User Journey Mapping to uncover friction points
Card Sorting & Information Architecture for structure
Delivered across 20+ screens, covering the full user journey.
This wasn’t just about scheduling a service.
The experience shifts from “I hope this gets fixed soon”to “I know exactly what’s happening and when.”

/projects/scalper.title: LaunchNow Portfolio Framer Template - From Analysis to Action: Trading on Charts
/projects/scalper.description: Designing intuitive digital experiences by simplifying complex problems—focusing on clarity, usability, and real-world impact across every product I build.
h1: From Analysis to Action: Trading on Charts
h2: Finding the rhythm of a trader’s mind
p: When markets move fast, seconds matter. Yet, most traders still juggle between their charting tool and order window — losing context, focus, and sometimes opportunity.That friction became the seed for this project — a new way to trade directly from charts, without ever leaving the market view.

This was a one-month sprint — and I was the sole UX designer, responsible for everything from research to the final prototype.The idea came up during a strategy discussion with the CPO: “What if traders could act instantly on what they see — right from the chart itself?”
Before diving into design, I needed to understand how traders think when markets are live.I spoke to five active traders and observed how they interacted with charts during real-time market hours.
Their reactions were clear and consistent:
• They hated switching tabs during volatility.
• They relied heavily on visual cues and quick access.
• They wanted control — but not clutter.

So, I built a user persona representing an active trader who juggles spot, futures, and options simultaneously — impatient with clutter but obsessed with control. Every design choice later tied back to this person’s need for speed, visibility, and confidence.
I began exploring how trade execution and chart interaction could coexist without fighting for space.
Early sketches looked chaotic — order window, positions on charts.It was easy to overwhelm the interface. So I stripped it back. The design had to breathe.
After a few iterations, a pattern started forming — a footer-first approach.Placing all trading actions in a footer panel kept the chart visible, yet gave traders instant access to buy/sell controls.
That balance — visibility and velocity — became the design’s foundation.

To make this work, I restructured the interface architecture.Charts took the central stage — occupying nearly 60% of the screen at all times.The rest of the elements: order book, open positions, and trade inputs — tucked neatly below or to the side as bottomsheets.
Every pixel served one principle: “The chart should never disappear.”
We already had a design library in place, ensuring visual consistency across the platform.
My focus was on refining trading interactions and clarity within the chart workspace — color-coded actions that kept the experience fast, focused, and trader-first.
To go deeper, I mapped emotions and actions through an empathy map.What do traders see during market swings? What do they hear from peers? What do they feel when the interface slows them down?
This helped reveal one truth — every extra click feels like a missed opportunity. That realization became the emotional anchor for every wireframe and design iteration that followed.
A quick glimpse of the high-fidelity screens showcasing key features.
(here comes some screenshots of the designs)

Once the prototype was ready, we tested it internally with professional traders who trade daily — the ones for whom every second counts. Their reactions were immediate and enthusiastic: they could place trades, adjust positions, and monitor charts without losing focus, all in one view.

Feedback like “This feels like trading on autopilot, but with full control” reinforced that our approach wasn’t just functional — it was transformative for their workflow. This internal validation gave us confidence that the design didn’t just meet expectations — it raised the bar for what chart-based trading could be, proving that a research-driven, user-focused approach really pays off.

/projects/orbital.title: LaunchNow Portfolio Framer Template - Understand your trade in seconds—not after calculations
/projects/orbital.description: Designing intuitive digital experiences by simplifying complex problems—focusing on clarity, usability, and real-world impact across every product I build.
h1: Understand your trade in seconds—not after calculations
h2: Seeing the whole story, at a glance
p: Designing a P&L curve that transforms scattered trading data into a clear, visual story—helping users understand how their positions evolve over time instead of relying on static numbers.

By combining a time-based graph with contextual signals like risk boundaries and real-time insights, the experience makes performance, movement, and decision points instantly understandable for both new and experienced traders.
By the end of this project, traders no longer had to interpret scattered numbers to understand their performance.
Instead, they could look at a single screen and instantly answer:
How is my trade performing right now?
Where did it go right—or wrong?
Am I within my risk limits?
What used to take multiple steps—switching between tables, calculations, and mental tracking—was reduced to a single, continuous visual experience.
Static numbers → Visual movement over time
Fragmented data → A single, unified view
At the center of this shift was a P&L curve—a graph that made performance intuitive, even for someone unfamiliar with trading.
Around it, key elements added clarity:
Risk boundaries through stop-loss and target lines
Real-time insights through a dedicated analytics panel
Context through best/worst performers and key metrics
Together, this created something more than a feature—a live “health view” of a trader’s positions.
Before this, traders had access to all the right data—but not in a way that was easy to understand.
Performance was spread across tables and numbers, making it hard to:
Track how a trade evolved during the day
Identify critical moments of profit or loss
The system answered what the numbers were, but not what they meant.
That gap became the foundation of this project.
The goal was not to add more information—but to make existing information effortless to understand.
The solution centered around a simple idea:
Turn trading data into a visual narrative.
A P&L curve showing profit and loss over time
Visual markers for key decision points (entry, stop loss, target)
Layered information architecture—graph first, details later
Every decision followed one principle:“A user should understand their position within seconds.”
One of the key challenges was designing for two very different users:
Someone who trades actively every day
The solution was to layer the experience:
The graph provides instant clarity (no expertise needed)
The analytics panel provides depth (for advanced users)
The data table ensures precision (for validation and detail)
This way, the interface doesn’t overwhelm beginners—but still respects the needs of experienced traders.
With this approach, the interaction changed fundamentally.

/projects/essentia.title: LaunchNow Portfolio Framer Template - Understand your entire financial year in one glance—not rows of data.
/projects/essentia.description: Designing intuitive digital experiences by simplifying complex problems—focusing on clarity, usability, and real-world impact across every product I build.
h1: Understand your entire financial year in one glance—not rows of data.
h2: Seeing a year of trading, clearly
Designing for both overview and depth
Part of a larger reporting ecosystem
p: Designing a realised P&L report that simplifies year-long trading data into a clear, visual experience—helping users quickly understand their overall performance without relying solely on complex tables.

By combining a financial year calendar view with detailed trade-level insights and flexible filtering, the solution bridges the gap between high-level understanding and deep analysis—making reporting both intuitive and actionable.

Designing a realised P&L report that transforms a traditionally dense, table-heavy experience into a visual and structured overview—helping users quickly understand how they performed across an entire financial year.

By combining a calendar-based view with detailed trade-level data, the experience allows users to both scan high-level performance instantly and dive deep when needed, making it useful for beginners as well as experienced traders.
By the end of this project, users no longer had to rely solely on spreadsheets or long tables to understand their yearly performance.
Instantly grasp profit or loss patterns through a color-coded financial calendar
View monthly net P&L summaries at a glance
Switch to a detailed table view for trade-level insights
Filter data across financial years, segments, exchanges, or symbols
Export specific reports for analysis or issue resolution
What was once overwhelming became structured, visual, and actionable.
For users, this meant less time interpreting data and more time making informed decisions.
Trading reports are often built for accuracy—but not always for understanding.
From user needs and platform context:
Users struggled to analyze performance across long timeframes
Financial year tracking lacked clear visual representation
Deep-dive analysis required switching between multiple tools
Support and issue resolution required exportable, filtered data
There was a clear gap between data availability and usability.
The core challenge:How might we help users understand a full year’s performance instantly, while still supporting detailed analysis when needed?
The solution was to create a dual-layer experience:
Monthly P&L summaries for quick insights
This layer answers: “How did I perform overall?”
Structured data for clarity and analysis
To make the report truly useful across different user needs, flexible filtering was introduced:
Beyond just viewing data, the report supports practical needs:
Helps users in filing issues or tracking specific trades
This made the feature not just informative—but functional in real workflows.
This project is one among 11 live reports on the platform—each solving different aspects of user needs.
Create a balance between visual simplicity and data depth
Contribute to a consistent reporting experience across the system
The experience shifts from “I need to figure this out”to “I can understand this instantly.”

/about.title: Mohan K
/about.description: Designing intuitive digital experiences by simplifying complex problems—focusing on clarity, usability, and real-world impact across every product I build.
h2: Design, travel, yoga, cars,and everything I enjoy beyond work.
p: I believe great design starts with understanding people, not just problems. At FYERS, I focus on creating simple, effective experiences by deeply understanding context before jumping into solutions.

Empathy drives my process, I look at problems from user, business, and technical perspectives to uncover meaningful insights. This helps me design experiences that are intuitive, relevant, and easy to use.

I care about crafting products that go beyond aesthetics, with a strong focus on usability, clarity, and real impact. For me, design is about understanding problems well enough to solve the right ones.
Trekking is where I feel most present and connected. It’s never just about reaching a summit, but about observing, learning, and reflecting along the way.
I’ve explored trails like Kodachadri, Kudremukha, Kumara Parvatha, and Kedarkantha each one teaching me patience, resilience, and the value of slowing down.
This curiosity extends into wildlife as well, where I spend time observing and capturing moments in nature shaping how I see the world with more awareness and respect.
Yoga began for me as a simple activity during my engineering days, but over time it became something more meaningful. It taught me the importance of breath, awareness, and being present.
I may not practice every day, but I return to it whenever I can. Exploring different asanas especially Dhanurasana it remains a quiet way for me to stay grounded and focused.
